title: FAA documents outline SpaceX plans for Starfall reentry vehicles
summary: FAA documents reveal SpaceX's plans to develop and test Starfall reentry vehicles for supporting in-space manufacturing projects. The project aims to advance SpaceX's capabilities in reusable spacecraft technology.

title: Blue Origin gets national security launch task order hours before New Glenn explosion
summary: Blue Origin received a national security launch task order from the U.S. Space Force and National Reconnaissance Office just hours before the explosion of its New Glenn rocket. The agencies reaffirmed their commitment to Blue Origin as a partner despite the incident.

title: Space Force awards SpaceX $4.16 billion to build satellite network for airborne target tracking
summary: The U.S. Space Force has awarded SpaceX a $4.16 billion contract to develop a low-orbit satellite constellation for tracking airborne threats like aircraft and cruise missiles. This initiative aims to enhance military surveillance capabilities.

title: Space Force’s commercial gatekeeper offers a playbook for startups seeking defense business
summary: The U.S. Space Force's commercial gatekeeper, Col. Tim Trimailo, advises startups to prioritize transparency, patience, and a clear military value proposition alongside technical innovation when seeking defense contracts. This guidance aims to help startups navigate the complexities of working with the Space Force.

title: Bellatrix and TelePIX plan 2028 air-breathing VLEO imaging demonstration
summary: TelePIX and Bellatrix Aerospace are collaborating on a 2028 demonstration mission to test air-breathing propulsion for very low Earth orbit (VLEO) imaging. The mission aims to advance geospatial imaging capabilities using innovative propulsion technology.

title: Spatial data has become a weapon of war in the US-Iran war
summary: Spatial data derived from satellites has become a critical weapon in the US-Iran conflict, highlighting the geospatial era of warfare. The blurring line between civilian and military satellite applications underscores the strategic importance of space-based information.

title: China’s latest batch of new and reusable rockets are close to launch
summary: China is preparing to launch a new batch of rockets, including reusable models, with debuts and recovery attempts planned in the coming months. Both state-led and commercial entities are involved in these upcoming space missions.

title: New Glenn rocket explodes on Cape Canaveral pad
summary: A Blue Origin New Glenn rocket exploded during a hotfire test at Cape Canaveral on May 28, destroying the rocket and causing significant pad damage.

title: Space Force plans nationwide network of ‘resilient operations centers’
summary: The U.S. Space Force is planning to establish a nationwide network of resilient operations centers to ensure military space missions can continue during wartime attacks. These facilities aim to enhance the survivability and operational continuity of critical space infrastructure.

title: Observable Space raises $90 million and wins Space Force contract for optical systems
summary: Observable Space raised $90 million and secured a U.S. Space Force contract to develop optical systems for laser communications and space domain awareness.

title: Can solar sails really send humans out into interstellar space?
summary: Solar sails are being considered as a potential technology to enable human travel to interstellar space, with experts suggesting the ideas are feasible rather than futuristic. The concept challenges traditional propulsion methods and could revolutionize deep-space exploration.

title: 'For All Mankind' star Wrenn Schmidt on her character's journey from NASA's mission control to the slammer (interview)
summary: Actress Wrenn Schmidt discusses her character's transformation from NASA mission control to prison in the series 'For All Mankind'. The interview highlights the character's exposure to new experiences while incarcerated.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 212 — Kabooms, Starship, and a Moon Base
summary: The latest episode of 'This Week In Space' discusses the loss of Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket, along with other topics like Starship and a Moon base.

title: Why is the idea of a Blue Moon so enchanting?
summary: The news article explores the cultural fascination with the concept of a 'Blue Moon', highlighting its romantic and inspirational appeal in human creativity and branding. It reflects on why this astronomical phenomenon has inspired songs, literature, and even product names.

title: Watch the smallest full moon of 2026 tonight in this rare 'Blue Moon' livestream
summary: The Virtual Telescope Project will livestream the smallest full moon of 2026, known as a 'Blue Moon,' starting at 9:30 p.m. EDT (0130 GMT) from Italy.

title: From 'Tetris' to 'StarCraft 2' — 5 times astronauts took video games into space
summary: Astronauts have taken video games like Tetris and StarCraft 2 into space for entertainment. This highlights the unique recreational activities enjoyed by astronauts during their missions.

title: The Blue Moon rises tonight: Where and when to see the second full moon of May
summary: The second full moon of May, known as the Blue Moon, will be visible tonight. This astronomical event occurs when there are two full moons in a single calendar month.

title: 'Star City': Release date & how to watch Apple TV's superb Soviet space series
summary: 'Star City' is a new Apple TV spinoff series set in the Soviet space program, offering a behind-the-scenes look at the Cold War-era space race. The series is part of the 'For All Mankind' universe and explores historical space missions.

title: NASA Awards Contract for Johnson Space Center Infrastructure
summary: NASA awarded a construction contract worth up to $300 million to seven companies for infrastructure upgrades at Johnson Space Center in Houston. The improvements include mission-support facilities, utilities, and equipment, with funds to be obligated by September 30, 2026.

title: NASA Hosts SpaceX Crew-11 Astronauts for Public Event at Headquarters
summary: NASA will host a public event featuring three astronauts from the SpaceX Crew-11 mission at its headquarters on June 1. The event will take place in the Webb Auditorium at NASA Headquarters in Washington, D.C.

title: NASA’s Roman Space Telescope Primary Mirror Gets Last Look
summary: NASA engineers completed the final inspection of the primary mirror for the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope. The 7.9-foot mirror will enable the telescope to capture wide-field images of the universe.

title: Hubble Spies Faint Irregular Galaxy
summary: The NASA Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the dwarf irregular galaxy ESO 490-017, located 23 million light-years away in the constellation Canis Major. The galaxy's low surface brightness makes it appear faint against brighter foreground stars.

title: Hubble Captures M88 on Journey to Center of Virgo Cluster
summary: The NASA Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the active spiral galaxy Messier 88 (M88), which is located approximately 63 million light-years away. M88 is part of the Virgo Cluster and is moving toward its center.

title: Painting the Growing Season in the Maize Triangle
summary: Radar data from an agricultural area in South Africa's Maize Triangle reveals crop types and seasonal changes using a vivid color palette. The data highlights variations in crop growth during the Southern Hemisphere's growing season.

title: NASA’s X-59 Prepares for First Supersonic Flight
summary: NASA's X-59 quiet supersonic research aircraft is gearing up for its first supersonic flight as part of a new block of test missions. The aircraft will also perform other critical mission objectives during these upcoming flights.

title: I Am Artemis: Daniel Stubbs
summary: NASA aerospace engineer Daniel Stubbs discusses the challenge of lunar dust obscuring visibility during Moon landings as part of the Artemis mission. The issue mirrors driving through a dust cloud on Earth, posing potential risks to human landing systems.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4900-4907: Pasadena, We Have a Drill Sample!
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover successfully drilled a sample at 'Campo Marte' on Mars, as confirmed by downlink data. The operation was part of ongoing science activities managed by the Jet Propulsion Laboratory.

title: NASA Uses Mineralogical Marker to Understand Ancient Martian Climate
summary: NASA's Curiosity Rover analyzed 20 Martian samples, finding that hematite crystallite size variations could indicate ancient climate changes on Mars.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn explodes during Static Fire test at LC-36
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered a catastrophic anomaly during a static fire test at Launch Complex-36, resulting in an explosion. This marks a significant setback for the company's orbital launch ambitions.

title: Rovers and Drones laying the foundations for NASA Moon Base
summary: NASA has outlined initial missions for its Moon Base initiative, involving rovers and drones to lay the groundwork. The initiative rebrands existing Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLPS) missions.

title: Following Starship V3 debut, SpaceX prepares for follow up
summary: SpaceX has completed the first flight of Starship Version 3 and is now preparing for a follow-up mission. The debut flight marks a significant milestone in the development of the next-generation Starship rocket.

title: Launch Preview: Starlink and Amazon Leo missions fill manifest
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled this week, with four of them being SpaceX's Starlink missions. Amazon's Leo satellite missions are also part of the manifest.

title: Vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance keep Expedition 74 busy through April
summary: Expedition 74 crew aboard the International Space Station has been occupied with vehicle arrivals, science experiments, and maintenance tasks throughout April. The activities highlight ongoing operations and research on the orbital outpost.

title: Shenzhou 23 launches new crew to Tiangong space station
summary: China launched the Shenzhou 23 mission to send a new crew to the Tiangong space station, which has been permanently occupied since June 2022.

title: Starship Flight 12: Block 3 Impresses on First Flight
summary: Starship Flight 12, featuring the Block 3 prototype, successfully launched on May 22, 2026, demonstrating impressive performance during its first flight. The mission marked a significant milestone for SpaceX's Starship program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Electron, and Vega C launch missions alongside Starship Flight 12
summary: Six space launches are scheduled worldwide in the next week, including SpaceX's 12th Starship flight, a Falcon 9 mission, an Electron launch, and a Vega C mission.

title: Vega C launches SMILE to study Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: ESA's SMILE mission launched aboard Vega C to study Earth's magnetosphere. The mission aims to enhance understanding of the Sun-Earth connection.

title: Super Heavy Block 3 the Booster of the Future
summary: The article discusses the evolution of the Super Heavy Block 3 booster, the first stage of SpaceX's Starship, highlighting its ongoing upgrades. It emphasizes the booster's role as a key component in future Starship missions.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of the European Space Agency's contributions to space exploration.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Researchers in ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section, including many young minds, test and develop materials to ensure spacecraft survive space conditions. Their work supports the success of ESA's missions by addressing harsh environmental challenges.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) to advance space exploration through training, operations, simulations, and public engagement. This initiative aims to enhance both technical and educational aspects of space missions.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test for a spacecraft's parachute system, involving a 'bake-out' process to ensure functionality. This highlights advancements in space mission technology and safety.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to avoid microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for Mars, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing and securing objects in space. This innovation could significantly enhance satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn Explodes on the Pad
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket experienced a catastrophic failure during its launch attempt, resulting in an explosion on the pad. The incident marks a significant setback for the company's orbital launch ambitions.

title: Europe Celebrates its Sovereignty Progress at SmallSat
summary: Europe highlighted its progress in achieving orbital sovereignty at SmallSat Europe. The event showcased advancements in independent space capabilities.

title: Observable Space Raises $90M Series A
summary: Observable Space, a space-tech startup, has secured $90 million in Series A funding to expand production of laser and optical hardware and grow internationally beyond the US.

title: Space Markets Emerges From Stealth With Coinbase Ventures Investment
summary: Space Markets has emerged from stealth mode with an investment from Coinbase Ventures. The company aims to create futures markets for space-related commodities and resources.

title: Exclusive: Stellar Alpina Raises $4.5M to Build Rotating Detonation Engines
summary: Stellar Alpina, a European propulsion startup, has raised $4.5 million to develop rotating detonation engines, a novel technology aimed at advancing spacecraft propulsion. The funding will support the company's efforts to bring this innovative thrust system to market.

title: Aitech Upgrades its Space Supercomputer
summary: Aitech has upgraded its space supercomputer by integrating NVIDIA's IGX Thor platform into the S-A2300 COTS AI Supercomputer, enhancing in-orbit data processing capabilities for customers.

title: NASA Awards LTV, Lunar Transport Contracts
summary: NASA has awarded contracts for the Lunar Terrain Vehicle (LTV) and lunar transport services, marking a significant step toward returning humans to the Moon. The announcement was made during a press conference led by NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman.

title: Op-ed: Commercial Demand in LEO is Lacking. The Government Can Help.
summary: A recent op-ed argues that the most promising market opportunity in Low Earth Orbit (LEO)—in-space manufacturing—currently lacks sufficient commercial demand to sustain itself, and calls for government support to drive growth.

title: Optical Links: When to Incorporate Them? When Not To?
summary: The article discusses the cost-effectiveness of optical links for satellite applications, noting that they often cost hundreds of thousands of dollars and may not be worth the investment in many cases.

title: Satellogic Lands $18M+ Defense-Monitoring Contract
summary: Satellogic secured a defense-monitoring contract worth over $18 million, marking its first major agreement following a strategic pivot to persistent-monitoring capabilities.

title: SpaceX Gets Nearly $4.2 Billion Nod From Space Force For Initial SB-AMTI
summary: SpaceX received a $4.2 billion contract from the U.S. Space Force for the initial phase of the SB-AMTI program. This follows a recent $2.3 billion award for the Space Data Network Backbone.

title: Sateliot Wins New Telenor IoT Deal
summary: Sateliot, a Spanish satellite company, has secured an IoT partnership with Telenor IoT, a division of the Nordic telecommunications giant Telenor Group. This collaboration expands Sateliot's reach in the satellite-based IoT market.

title: Viasat Full Year ‘26 Takeaways: Record Revenue, Equatys Moves Forward
summary: Viasat reported record revenue and awards for the full year 2026, but did not meet stabilization goals in maritime and fixed broadband sectors. The company also highlighted progress on its Equatys initiative.

title: Blue Origin’s Launch Pad Explosion Could Impact Timelines for Commercial Customers, NASA Missions
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket suffered a launch pad explosion at Cape Canaveral, potentially delaying commercial and NASA missions. The incident marks a significant setback for the company's ambitious spaceflight timelines.

title: EU Targets User Terminal Tech Advancements Ahead of IRIS²
summary: The European Union has issued a new tender for innovative and cost-effective user terminal technologies for the IRIS² satellite constellation. This initiative aims to advance user terminal tech ahead of the constellation's deployment.

title: BlackSky Advances Non-Earth Imaging Business With 7-Figure Renewal
summary: BlackSky Technology secured a seven-figure contract renewal for non-Earth imagery services, capturing images of objects in orbit. This advancement strengthens their business in space situational awareness.

title: UK’s O2 Satellite Expands D2D Service to iPhones
summary: Virgin Media O2's direct-to-device (D2D) service O2 Satellite now supports iPhones, expanding its reach. The service leverages SpaceX’s Starlink Mobile constellation, which launched earlier this year.

title: Swissto12 Adds to Leadership Team to Target Global Growth
summary: Swissto12 has expanded its leadership team by appointing a new chief commercial officer (CCO) and executives focused on government affairs and partnerships to drive global expansion. The company, which specializes in satellite technology, is positioning itself for increased international growth.

title: Orbital Data Centers Must Tackle Chip Lifespan, Launch Availability, and Cybersecurity Challenges
summary: Orbital data centers face challenges like chip lifespan, launch availability, and cybersecurity. The topic gained attention following SpaceX's initial IPO documents.

title: Will the European Launch Market Finally Ignite?
summary: The European space industry is grappling with the need for more efficient and reliable access to space, a topic that dominated a recent launch panel discussion. The question of when the European launch market will see significant progress remains unresolved.
